---
title: "Persona-2"
author: Ixel
format: html

---

Local and regional government practitioners (city or county analysts)
Who: A city or county specialist, like a landscape ecologist hired by a city.
Want: Subset to their jurisdiction (Santa Barbara city or county), run local exploration and sensitivity tests, and potentially swap in better local datasets.
Why: They do not want to manage the whole stack, and they cannot do billion pixel scale computation on a laptop.
Key traits: Wants “pull my area and run it here,” plus the ability to replace generic layers with higher quality local ones (example: field assessed defensible space).
 Cfong_nceas


Core extraction functions:

get_wri_scores(geography = "Santa Barbara City") - Pull all domain scores for their jurisdiction
extract_layer(layer = "defensible_space", boundary = shapefile) - Get a specific layer clipped to their area
compare_to_peers(my_area, peer_areas) - Benchmark against similar cities/counties

Sensitivity testing functions:

replace_layer(domain = "Communities", layer = "defensible_space", new_data = my_raster) - Swap in their local data
recalculate_score(domain = "Communities", modified_layers) - See how score changes with local data
sensitivity_report(layer_variations) - Test multiple "what if" scenarios

Exploration functions:

summarize_scores(geography, by = "census_tract") - Break down city-level score into neighborhoods
map_domain(domain = "Habitats", geography) - Quick visualization
export_for_presentation(geography, format = "shapefile") - Get data ready for ArcGIS/QGIS

Simple wrappers:

get_city_report(city_name) - One function that pulls everything and creates summary tables/maps
list_available_layers(domain) - See what can be replaced

Key principle: Each function should do one clear thing. Avoid functions that require users to understand the full WRI methodology.
